importantVotes.title: Hearst Hotel Tax Incentive Agreement – Up to $40M
importantVotes.description: Approves a 20-year agreement giving Bespoke Hospitality up to $40 million in net present value from new hotel occupancy taxes for the proposed Hearst Hotel at 5 Third Street. The deal subsidizes hotel development in the mid-Market area. Chan and Fielder voted no.

importantVotes.title: Re-Affirming City Support to Acquire PG&E Assets
importantVotes.description: Reaffirms the Board's support for the City acquiring PG&E's local electrical distribution infrastructure, following a series of major PG&E outages that disrupted power across San Francisco neighborhoods.

importantVotes.title: Urging CPUC to Hold PG&E Accountable for Outages
importantVotes.description: Urges Governor Newsom and the California Public Utilities Commission to hold PG&E accountable for repeated power outages across San Francisco. Passed alongside a parallel resolution supporting the City acquiring PG&E assets.

importantVotes.title: Bay Area Urban Areas Security Initiative MOU
importantVotes.description: Retroactively approves a regional agreement with Oakland, San Jose, and Bay Area counties governing how federal Urban Areas Security Initiative (UASI) homeland security grant funds are distributed to the Bay Area.

importantVotes.title: Fixed Two-Year Budget for Airport, Port, and PUC
importantVotes.description: Requires the Airport, Port, and Public Utilities Commission to submit fixed two-year budgets for FY2026-27 and FY2027-28, aligning them with the City's broader biennial budget cycle.

importantVotes.title: Paratransit Services Contract Extension – $339M Total
importantVotes.description: Extends Transdev Services' paratransit brokerage contract by two years through June 2028, increasing the total contract value by $30.8 million to $339 million. Paratransit provides accessible transit for people with disabilities.

importantVotes.title: Up to $150M in Commercial Paper for Capital Projects
importantVotes.description: Reauthorizes up to $150 million in tax-exempt and taxable lease revenue commercial paper certificates to fund city capital improvements and equipment acquisitions.

importantVotes.title: Vitalant Blood Products Contract Extension – $28.2M
importantVotes.description: Extends the Department of Public Health's blood and blood products contract with Vitalant by five years through September 2031, increasing the total contract by $18.3 million to $28.2 million.
